description: He starts seeing things before they happen. At first, it’s subtle. A moment that feels out of place. A flicker of something just before it occurs. Then it happens again. And again. John can’t explain it. He only knows something isn’t right. Time seems to hesitate. Events shift. And beneath it all, there’s a growing sense that he is not alone. Then he sees it. Not a memory. Not a dream. Something watching him. Waiting. As the gaps in reality widen, John is drawn into a mystery that refuses to stay in the past, present, or future. Because whatever is reaching through… already knows him. And it’s getting closer. What You Can’t See is a tense, atmospheric sci-fi thriller about time, identity, and the unsettling possibility that some moments are not meant to stay where they belong. Perfect for readers who enjoy: Mind-bending, time-slip thrillers Psychological mysteries with an eerie edge Stories in the vein of Blake Crouch and unsettling speculative fiction Scroll up and get your copy today — before reality changes again. Read more
